column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en#audio_url

BG 8.B#ॐ श्री परमात्मने नमः
अथ अष्टमोऽध्यायः
अक्षर ब्रह्म योगः#om sri paramatmane namaha
atha ashtamodhyayaha
akshara brahma yogaha#Salutations to the most auspicious divine in all. Henceforth, the eighth chapter named Akshara Brahma Yoga#om sri paramatmane—the most auspicious divine in all
column5: akshara brahma yogaha—Yoga of the eternal Brahman#

column1:
BG 8.1#अर्जुन उवाच |
किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं
किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम |
अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तम्
अधिदैवं किमुच्यते ||1||#arjuna uvācha
kiṁ tad brahma kim adhyātmaṁ
kiṁ karma puruṣhottama
adhibhūtaṁ cha kiṁ proktam
adhidaivaṁ kim uchyate#Arjuna asked: O Supreme Person (Purushottama), what is Brahman, what is the self (Adhyatma), and what is action (Karma)? What is said to constitute the material manifestation (Adhibhuta), and what is called the divine realm (Adhidaiva)?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said
column9: puruṣha-uttama—Shree Krishna, the Supreme Divine Personality
column14: adhidaivam—the Lord of the celestial gods

column1:
BG 8.2#अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र
देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन |
प्रयाणकाले च कथं
ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ||2||#adhiyajñaḥ kathaṁ ko ’tra
dehe ’smin madhusūdana
prayāṇa-kāle cha kathaṁ
jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ#O Madhusudana, who is the Lord of Sacrifice (Adhiyajna), and how does He reside within this body? Furthermore, how can those with self-control know You at the final moment of death?#adhiyajñaḥ—the Lord all sacrificial performances
column7: madhusūdana—Shree Krishna, the killer of the demon named Madhu

column1:
BG 8.3#श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं
स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते |
भूतभावोद्भवकरः
विसर्गः कर्मसञ्ज्ञितः ||3||#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
akṣharaṁ brahma paramaṁ
svabhāvo ’dhyātmam uchyate
bhūta-bhāvodbhava-karo
visargaḥ karma-sanjñitaḥ#The Lord said: The Supreme Indestructible Entity is called Brahman
column2: one’s own self is called adhyatma. Actions pertaining to the material personality of living beings, and its development are called karma, or fruitive activities.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Lord said
column9: bhūta-bhāva-udbhava-karaḥ—actions pertaining to the material personality of living beings, and its development

column1:
BG 8.4#अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः
पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् |
अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र
देहे देहभृतां वर ||4||#adhibhūtaṁ kṣharo bhāvaḥ
puruṣhaśh chādhidaivatam
adhiyajño ’ham evātra
dehe deha-bhṛitāṁ vara#O best of the embodied souls, the physical manifestation that is constantly changing is called adhibhūta
column2: the universal form of God, which presides over the celestial gods in this creation, is called Adhidaiva
column3: I, who dwell in the heart of every living being, am called Adhiyajna, or the Lord of all sacrifices.#adhibhūtam—the ever changing physical manifestation
column6: puruṣhaḥ—the cosmic personality of God, encompassing the material creation
column8: adhidaivatam—the Lord of the celestial gods

column1:
BG 8.5#अन्तकाले च मामेव
स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् |
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं
याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ||5||#anta-kāle cha mām eva
smaran muktvā kalevaram
yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ
yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ#Those who relinquish the body while remembering Me at the moment of death will come to Me. There is certainly no doubt about this.#anta-kāle—at the time of death

BG 8.6#यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं
त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् |
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय
सदा तद्भावभावितः ||6||#yaṁ yaṁ vāpi smaran bhāvaṁ
tyajatyante kalevaram
taṁ tam evaiti kaunteya
sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ#Whatever one remembers upon giving up the body at the time of death, O son of Kunti, one attains that state, being always absorbed in such contemplation.#yam yam—whatever

BG 8.7#तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु
मामनुस्मर युध्य च |
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिः
मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम् ||7||#tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu
mām anusmara yudhya cha
mayyarpita-mano-buddhir
mām evaiṣhyasyasanśhayam#Therefore, always remember Me and also do your duty of fighting the war. With mind and intellect surrendered to Me, you will definitely attain Me
column2: of this, there is no doubt.#tasmāt—therefore

column1:
BG 8.8#अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन
चेतसा नान्यगामिना |
परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं
याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ||8||#abhyāsa-yoga-yuktena
chetasā nānya-gāminā
paramaṁ puruṣhaṁ divyaṁ
yāti pārthānuchintayan#With practice, O Parth, when you constantly engage the mind in remembering Me, the Supreme Divine Personality, without deviating, you will certainly attain Me.#abhyāsa-yoga—by practice of yog

BG 8.9#कविं पुराणमनुशासितारम्
अणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः |
सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूपम्
आदित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ||9||#kaviṁ purāṇam anuśhāsitāram
aṇor aṇīyānsam anusmared yaḥ
sarvasya dhātāram achintya-rūpam
āditya-varṇaṁ tamasaḥ parastāt#One should meditate on the Supreme Divine as the omniscient, ancient Lord, the ruler of all, subtler than the subtlest, the sustainer of everything, possessing an inconceivable form, radiant like the sun, and entirely beyond all darkness.#kavim—poet

BG 8.10#प्रयाणकाले मनसाचलेन
भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव |
भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्
स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ||10||#prayāṇa-kāle manasāchalena
bhaktyā yukto yoga-balena chaiva
bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśhya samyak
sa taṁ paraṁ puruṣham upaiti divyam#At the time of death, one who with an unmoving mind, filled with devotion and the power of yoga, perfectly establishes the life-force (prana) between the eyebrows, attains that Supreme Divine Person.#prayāṇa-kāle—at the time of death

BG 8.11#यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति
विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः |
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति
तत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ||11||#yad akṣharaṁ veda-vido vadanti
viśhanti yad yatayo vīta-rāgāḥ
yad ichchhanto brahmacharyaṁ charanti
tat te padaṁ sangraheṇa pravakṣhye#Scholars of the Vedas describe Him as Imperishable
column2: great ascetics practice the vow of celibacy and renounce worldly pleasures to enter into Him. I shall now explain to you briefly the path to that goal.#yat—which

column1:
BG 8.12#सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य
मनो हृदि निरुध्य च |
मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणम्
आस्थितो योगधारणाम् ||12||#sarva-dvārāṇi sanyamya
mano hṛidi nirudhya cha
mūrdhnyādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam
āsthito yoga-dhāraṇām#Restraining all the gates of the body and fixing the mind in the heart region, and then drawing the life-breath to the head, one should get established in steadfast yogic concentration.#sarva-dvārāṇi—all gates

BG 8.13#ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म
व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् |
यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं
स याति परमां गतिम् ||13||#oṁ ityekākṣharaṁ brahma
vyāharan mām anusmaran
yaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṁ
sa yāti paramāṁ gatim#One who departs from the body while remembering Me, the Supreme Personality, and chanting the syllable Om, will attain the supreme goal.#om—sacred syllable representing the formless aspect of God

BG 8.14#अनन्यचेताः सततं
यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः |
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ
नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ||14||#ananya-chetāḥ satataṁ
yo māṁ smarati nityaśhaḥ
tasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha
nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ#O Parth, for those yogis who always think of Me with exclusive devotion, I am easily attainable because of their constant absorption in Me.#ananya-chetāḥ—without deviation of the mind

BG 8.15#मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म
दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् |
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः
संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ||15||#mām upetya punar janma
duḥkhālayam aśhāśhvatam
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ
sansiddhiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ#Having attained Me, the great souls are no more subject to rebirth in this world, which is transient and full of misery, because they have attained the highest perfection.#mām—Me

BG 8.16#आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः
पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन |
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय
पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ||16||#ā-brahma-bhuvanāl lokāḥ
punar āvartino ’rjuna
mām upetya tu kaunteya
punar janma na vidyate#In all the worlds of this material creation, up to the highest abode of Brahma, you will be subject to rebirth, O Arjun. But on attaining My Abode, O son of Kunti, there is no further rebirth.#ā-brahma-bhuvanāt—up to the abode of Brahma

BG 8.17#सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तम्
अहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः |
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां
तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ||17||#sahasra-yuga-paryantam
ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ
rātriṁ yuga-sahasrāntāṁ
te ’ho-rātra-vido janāḥ#One day of Brahma (kalp) lasts a thousand cycles of the four ages (mahā yug) and his night also extends for the same span of time. The wise who know this understand the reality about day and night.#sahasra—one thousand
column11: ahaḥ-rātra-vidaḥ—those who know this day and night

column1:
BG 8.18#अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः
प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे |
रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते
तत्रैवाव्यक्तसञ्ज्ञके ||18||#avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ
prabhavantyahar-āgame
rātryāgame pralīyante
tatraivāvyakta-sanjñake#At the advent of Brahma’s day, all living beings emanate from the unmanifest source. And at the fall of his night, all embodied beings again merge into their unmanifest source.#avyaktāt—from the unmanifested
column5: ahaḥ-āgame—at the advent of Brahma’s day
column6: rātri-āgame—at the fall of Brahma’s night
column10: avyakta-sanjñake—in that which is called the unmanifest#

column1:
BG 8.19#भूतग्रामः स एवायं
भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते |
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ
प्रभवत्यहरागमे ||19||#bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṁ
bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate
rātryāgame ’vaśhaḥ pārtha
prabhavatyahar-āgame#Multitudes of beings repeatedly take birth with the advent of Brahma’s day, and are reabsorbed on the arrival of the cosmic night, to manifest again automatically on the advent of the next cosmic day.#bhūta-grāmaḥ—the multitude of beings

BG 8.20#परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्यः
अव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः |
यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु
नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ||20||#paras tasmāt tu bhāvo ’nyo
’vyakto ’vyaktāt sanātanaḥ
yaḥ sa sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu
naśhyatsu na vinaśhyati#Transcendental to this manifest and unmanifest creation, there is yet another unmanifest eternal dimension. That realm does not cease even when all others do.#paraḥ—transcendental

BG 8.21#अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तः
तमाहुः परमां गतिम् |
यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते
तद्धाम परमं मम ||21||#avyakto ’kṣhara ityuktas
tam āhuḥ paramāṁ gatim
yaṁ prāpya na nivartante
tad dhāma paramaṁ mama#That unmanifest dimension is the supreme goal, and upon reaching it, one never returns to this mortal world. That is My Supreme Abode.#avyaktaḥ—unmanifest

BG 8.22#पुरुषः स परः पार्थ
भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया |
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि
येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ||22||#puruṣhaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha
bhaktyā labhyas tvananyayā
yasyāntaḥ-sthāni bhūtāni
yena sarvam idaṁ tatam#The Supreme Divine Personality is greater than all that exists. Although He is all-pervading and all living beings are situated in Him, yet He can be known only through devotion.#puruṣhaḥ—the Supreme Divine Personality

BG 8.23#यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिम्
आवृत्तिं चैव योगिनः |
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं
वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ||23||#yatra kāle tvanāvṛittim
āvṛittiṁ chaiva yoginaḥ
prayātā yānti taṁ kālaṁ
vakṣhyāmi bharatarṣhabha#O best of the Bharatas (Arjuna), I shall now explain to you the different times at which departs a yogi to either attain the path of no return (liberation) or the path of return (rebirth).#yatra—where
column14: bharata-ṛiṣhabha—Arjun, the best of the Bharatas

column1:
BG 8.24#अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः
षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् |
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति
ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ||24||#agnir jyotir ahaḥ śhuklaḥ
ṣhaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam
tatra prayātā gachchhanti
brahma brahma-vido janāḥ#Those who know the Supreme Brahman and depart from this world during the influence of the fiery light, the day, the bright fortnight of the moon, and the six months of the sun's northern course, attain that Supreme Brahman.#agniḥ—fire
column4: śhuklaḥ—the bright fortnight of the moon

column1:
BG 8.25#धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः
षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् |
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिः
योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ||25||#dhūmo rātris tathā kṛiṣhṇaḥ
ṣhaṇ-māsā dakṣhiṇāyanam
tatra chāndramasaṁ jyotir
yogī prāpya nivartate#Departing from this world during the influence of smoke, the night, the dark fortnight of the moon, and the six months of the sun's southern course, a yogi reaches the lunar light and is destined to return (be reborn).#dhūmaḥ—smoke
column4: kṛiṣhṇaḥ—the dark fortnight of the moon

column1:
BG 8.26#शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते
जगतः शाश्वते मते |
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिम्
अन्ययावर्तते पुनः ||26||#śhukla-kṛiṣhṇe gatī hyete
jagataḥ śhāśhvate mate
ekayā yātyanāvṛittim
anyayāvartate punaḥ#These two paths of the world—the bright and the dark—are considered eternal
column2: by the former, one ascends to the path of no return (liberation), while by the latter, one returns to the cycle of rebirth.#śhukla—bright

column1:
BG 8.27#नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्
योगी मुह्यति कश्चन |
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु
योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ||27||#naite sṛitī pārtha jānan
yogī muhyati kaśhchana
tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu
yoga-yukto bhavārjuna#Yogis who know the secret of these two paths, O Parth, are never bewildered. Therefore, at all times be situated in Yog (union with God).#na—never

BG 8.28#वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् |
अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ||28||#vedeṣhu yajñeṣhu tapaḥsu chaiva
dāneṣhu yat puṇya-phalaṁ pradiṣhṭam
atyeti tat sarvam idaṁ viditvā
yogī paraṁ sthānam upaiti chādyam#The yogis, who know this secret, gain merit far beyond the fruits of Vedic rituals, the study of the Vedas, performance of sacrifices, austerities, and charities. Such yogis reach the Supreme Abode.#vedeṣhu—in the study of the Vedas

BG 8.E#ॐ तत् सत् इति श्रीमत् भगवत् गीतासु उपनिषत्सु
ब्रह्म-विद्यायां योग-शास्त्रे श्रीकृष्ण-अर्जुन-संवादे
अक्षर-ब्रह्म योगो नाम अष्टमोऽध्यायः#Om tat sat iti
Srimad Bhagavad Gitasu Upanishatsu
Brahma Vidyayam yogashastre Sri-Krishna-arujuna samvade
Akshara Brahma Yogo Nama ashtamodhyaha#Om Truth Is. Thus in the Upanishads of the glorious Bhagavad Gita, the science of the Eternal, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna ends the Eighth discourse entitled The Yoga of the Eternal Brahman.#Om—Om
column11: Sri Krishna arujuna samvade—a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna
column12: Akshara Brahma Yogo Nama—Yoga of the Eternal Brahman
